macro_segments.title: Textual Ambiguities in Deuteronomy and Joshua Regarding the Stones and the Torah Writing

micro_segments.title: Correction and Review of the Pesukim on Stone Erection and Torah Writing
Three Sets of Stones: Those of Moshe in the Jordan, Those Taken from the Jordan and Erected in Gilgal, and Those at Har Gerizim and Har Eival
Detailed Analysis of Deuteronomy 27: Erecting Stones, Plastering, Writing the Torah, Building the Mizbeiach, and the Brachot and Klalot
Tosafot's Distinction Between Stones at the Malon and Stones of the Mizbeiach, and the Role of Non-Jewish Scribes
The Distinction Between Two Stages: Erection of Stones at Gilgal Versus the Mizbeiach, Brachot, and Klalot at Har Gerizim and Har Eival in Joshua
Geographic Analysis: The Distance from Gilgal to Har Gerizim and Har Eival, and the Timeline of Events in Joshua
The Malbim's Approach: Multiple Writings on the Plaster at Different Times

macro_segments.title: Teshuvah, Acceptance, and the Seven Nations: Dispute Between Rabbi Yehudah and Rabbi Shimon

micro_segments.title: Rava's Explanation of Rabbi Shimon's Position: Learning from the Written Torah Could Have Led to Teshuvah and Acceptance
Rabbi Yehudah's Opposing View: Like Plaster Which Cannot Be Fixed, the Seven Nations Must Be Destroyed Unconditionally
The Baraita on Yefat Toar: The Inclusion of Canaanites Outside the Land If They Do Teshuvah
Tosafot's Discussion of Rachav and the Timing of Teshuvah: Before Versus After War
Tosafot's Question: Does Yefat Toar Override the Mitzvah to Destroy the Seven Nations?

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Harmonization: How All Events Occurred on One Day with Miraculous Transportation

micro_segments.title: Bo Re'eh: How Many Miracles Were Performed—the Journey of Sixty Mil to Har Gerizim and Har Eival in One Day
The Bracketing of the Yericho and Ha'Ai Battles: Ein Mukdam u'Meuchar and the Miraculous Transportation
The Fear Sent Before Them: The Verse on Emah and the Disturbance of the Bowels, and the Distinction Between the First and Second Entries
The Gemara's Solution: Taking the Stones from the Jordan, Bringing Them to Har Gerizim and Har Eival, Building the Mizbeiach, Writing the Torah, and Then Returning Them to Gilgal
The Resolution of Ambiguities: The Gemara's Synthesis of the Devarim and Yehoshua Narratives Regarding Where the Torah Was Written
The Issue of the Tzira (Wasps): Did They Cross Into Eretz Yisrael, or Did They Stand at the Edge of the Jordan?
Rav Papa's Resolution: Two Tzira—One of Moshe (Did Not Cross) and One of Yehoshua (Did Cross)

macro_segments.title: The Division of the Tribes: Har Gerizim Versus Har Eival

micro_segments.title: Six Tribes on Har Gerizim and Six on Har Eival: The Order and the Meaning of Ve'hachetzyo
Rav Kahana's Answer: The Division Reflects the Stones of the Ephod, Where Six Tribe Names Were on One Stone and Six on the Other
The Baraita on the Ephod: Twenty-Five Letters on Each Stone, with the Second Stone Following Birth Order Rather Than the First
Rabbi Chanina ben Gamliel's Disagreement: The Order Should Follow the Listing in Shemot, Not Bamidbar
The Gemara's Question: Ve'hachetzyo Does Not Refer to the Division of the Stones but to Something Else
The Baraita's Solution: The Half on Har Gerizim Was Larger in Number Because Levi Was Below (on Har Eival)
The Role of Yosef: His Tribe's Large Population Made Up for the Levi Deficit on Har Gerizim
Tosafot's Calculation: Adding Up the Census Numbers from the End of Bamidbar to Verify the Division
The Issue of Fifty Letters: The Gemara Counts 49 Letters (25 and 24), Not 50
Rabbi Yitzchak's Solution: Yosef Is Written with an Extra Heh (Yehosef) in One Verse, Making the Count 50 with 25 and 25
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's Counter: Binyamin Is Written Shalem (With Yud) Only When Born, Satisfying Ketoldotam

macro_segments.title: The Story of Yosef: His Resistance to Potiphar's Wife and the Connection to the Ephod Stones

micro_segments.title: Rav Chana bar Bizna in the Name of Rabbi Shimon Chasida: Yosef Sanctified God's Name in Private and Received the Heh from God's Name
The Comparison: Yehudah Sanctified God's Name Publicly and Is Called Entirely by God's Name
The Narrative of Temptation: Yosef's Intent and Potiphar's Wife's Scheme on a Day When the Household Visited a Temple of Idolatry
The Image of Yaakov: The Appearance of Yosef's Father in the Window to Dissuade Him, Reminding Him of His Name on the Ephod Stones
Yosef's Physical Resistance: Placing His Strength in His Bow and Pressing His Fingers into the Ground
The Result: Yosef Becomes a Shepherd of Israel, Fulfilling His Role as Bearer of the Divine Name

Ein Mukdam u'Meuchar (No Early or Late in Torah)
